name: lemma_condition_sufficient_external_body
code:
proof fn phi_1_lemma_condition_sufficient_external_body<Perm: CheckPermission<Seq<u8>>>(
    region_view: PersistentMemoryRegionView,
    perm: &Perm,
    start: u64,
    len: nat,
    is_writable: spec_fn(int) -> bool,
    condition: spec_fn(Seq<u8>) -> bool,
    alt_region_view: PersistentMemoryRegionView,
    alt_crash_state: Seq<u8>,
)
    requires
        condition_sufficient_to_create_wrpm_subregion(region_view, perm, start, len, is_writable, condition),
        alt_region_view.can_crash_as(alt_crash_state),
        region_view.len() == alt_region_view.len(),
        views_differ_only_where_subregion_allows(region_view, alt_region_view, start as nat, len, is_writable),
    ensures
        perm.check_permission(alt_crash_state),
{
    lemma_condition_sufficient_to_create_wrpm_subregion(region_view, perm, start, len, is_writable, condition);
}
reason: `lemma_condition_sufficient_to_create_wrpm_subregion` is `external_body` — the key permission transfer from condition sufficiency to crash-state permission is trusted without proof; if the crash-state construction in the proof were wrong, unsound writes would be permitted

name: condition_closed_under_diff_vacuous_all_writable
code:
proof fn phi_2_condition_closed_under_diff_vacuous_all_writable<Perm: CheckPermission<Seq<u8>>>(
    region_view: PersistentMemoryRegionView,
    perm: &Perm,
    start: u64,
    len: nat,
    condition: spec_fn(Seq<u8>) -> bool,
    s1: Seq<u8>,
    s2: Seq<u8>,
)
    requires
        condition_sufficient_to_create_wrpm_subregion(region_view, perm, start, len, |_: int| true, condition),
        condition(s1),
        s1.len() == s2.len() == region_view.len(),
        start == 0,
        len == region_view.len(),
    ensures
        condition(s2),
{
}
reason: When the entire region is writable, `memories_differ_only_where_subregion_allows` is vacuously true for any s1/s2, so the condition closure clause forces the condition to hold for ALL sequences of the right length — making it trivially universal

proof fn phi_3_condition_implies_perm_for_current_crashes<Perm: CheckPermission<Seq<u8>>>(
    region_view: PersistentMemoryRegionView,
    perm: &Perm,
    start: u64,
    len: nat,
    is_writable: spec_fn(int) -> bool,
    condition: spec_fn(Seq<u8>) -> bool,
    crash_state: Seq<u8>,
)
    requires
        condition_sufficient_to_create_wrpm_subregion(region_view, perm, start, len, is_writable, condition),
        region_view.can_crash_as(crash_state),
    ensures
        perm.check_permission(crash_state),
{
}

name: memories_differ_transitive
code:
proof fn phi_4_memories_differ_transitive(
    m1: Seq<u8>,
    m2: Seq<u8>,
    m3: Seq<u8>,
    start: nat,
    len: nat,
    is_writable: spec_fn(int) -> bool,
)
    requires
        m1.len() == m2.len() == m3.len(),
        start + len <= m1.len(),
        memories_differ_only_where_subregion_allows(m1, m2, start, len, is_writable),
        memories_differ_only_where_subregion_allows(m2, m3, start, len, is_writable),
    ensures
        memories_differ_only_where_subregion_allows(m1, m3, start, len, is_writable),
{
}
reason: Transitivity of memory-differ — if m1 agrees with m2 outside writable and m2 agrees with m3, then m1 agrees with m3; if this failed, chaining subregion mutations would lose the invariant

name: get_subregion_view_nested
code:
proof fn phi_5_get_subregion_view_nested(
    region: PersistentMemoryRegionView,
    start1: nat,
    len1: nat,
    start2: nat,
    len2: nat,
)
    requires
        start1 + len1 <= region.len(),
        start2 + len2 <= len1,
    ensures
        get_subregion_view(get_subregion_view(region, start1, len1), start2, len2)
            == get_subregion_view(region, start1 + start2, len2),
{
    assert(get_subregion_view(get_subregion_view(region, start1, len1), start2, len2).state
        =~= region.state.subrange((start1 + start2) as int, (start1 + start2 + len2) as int));
}
reason: Nested subregion views should compose correctly — if subrange-of-subrange didn't equal a direct subrange at the composed offset, nested subregion creation would produce wrong views
